# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Blob Storage to store application logs and backups. Configure for standard performance tier with default access settings. Skip lifecycle management policies for now to keep the template simple. This is for our logging infrastructure.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

resources: 
- name: storageAccountResourceGroup
  type: Microsoft.Resources/resourceGroups@2019-07-01
  properties: 
    location: {your_location} # Example value 'West Europe' or a valid Azure region e.g., North Central US, West CentralUS etc...  
- name: storageAccountNameResourceGroup
  type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2019-07-01   
  dependsOn: ['storageAccountResourceGroup']        # Dependency to create resource group first before creating a Storage account    
  properties:     
    location: '{your_location}'           # Same as above. Example value is same in step mentioned earlier  
    kind: StandardLRS                # The type of the storage (Standard for LRS) e.g., Premium, Hot or Cold HDDs and not yet Caching Options      
- name: blobStorageResourceGroup        
  type: Microsoft.Resources/resourceGroups@2019-07-01  
  properties:   
     location: '{your_location}'        # Same as above in the first step (Example value is same). It will be a valid Azure region e,g., North Central US etc...     
- name: storageContainerName        
  type : Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2019-07-01   
  dependsOn:'[blobStorageResourceGroup]' # dependency to create the container first before creating an Storage account       
  properties:    
   location: '{your_location}'       # Same as above in step mentioned earlier. It will be a valid Azure region e,g., North Central US etc...     
   resourceGroupName : {storageAccountNameResourceGroup}           # The name of the previous resources group which is created first before creating an storage account container       
-  properties:   
     createEmptyContainer: true         # Setting this property to TRUE will automatically make a new empty blob容器 when you upload any data. You can delete or change its value later if needed     
   type : Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2019-07-01       
  name:'{your_resourceGroup}'           # Same as above in step mentioned earlier    It will be a valid Azure resource group e,g., MyResourceGroupName etc...
